items.title: The appeal of AI is agency, not magic.
items.tickerTitle: The appeal of AI is agency, not magic
items.summary: A personal launch note for Appeal of AI: the positive case for useful machine intelligence without pretending the hard parts are small.

items.title: Claude Fable 5 makes the model release feel like a governance story.
items.tickerTitle: Claude Fable 5 turns safeguards into the story
items.summary: A field note on why the newest Claude coverage is not only about capability, but about what kind of trust a model tries to earn.
items.xPost: A frontier model launch is no longer only a capability story. The sharper signal is the trust wrapper: access, refusals, safeguards, and the public performance of control.

items.title: The AI upside needs a public deal.
items.tickerTitle: The AI upside needs a public deal
items.summary: A personal note on AI, work, public upside and why the appeal of the technology depends on how broadly its gains are shared.
items.xPost: The appeal of AI gets weaker if the upside feels privately captured and publicly imposed. The public deal matters: access, education, worker mobility, provenance, creator rights, and useful transparency.

items.title: Anthropic's adolescence warning is really about growing up in public.
items.tickerTitle: Anthropic frames AI as the adolescence of technology
items.summary: Dario Amodei's warning works because it treats AI as powerful, immature and already socially consequential.
items.xPost: The useful part of Anthropic's adolescence frame is not the drama. It is the admission that AI is powerful, immature and already changing the room. That is exactly the phase where confidence is easy and judgment matters.

items.title: AI slop is exhausting the public internet.
items.tickerTitle: AI slop is exhausting the public internet
items.summary: A personal note on synthetic comments, generic article filler and why people can feel when language has no witness behind it.
items.xPost: AI slop is exhausting because it has the shape of participation without the weight of having participated. The better line is taste: use the tool, but leave a fingerprint.

items.title: Grok Build makes the coding-agent race feel official.
items.tickerTitle: Grok puts the coding-agent race inside the terminal
items.summary: xAI's Grok Build Beta turns Grok into a terminal coding agent, while fresh safety questions keep the tool story from becoming pure hype.
items.xPost: Grok Build is less interesting as one CLI and more interesting as a signal: coding agents are becoming platform surfaces. The model wants the repo, terminal, plan, diff and review. The safety questions need to sit right next to the excitement.

items.title: Messi and OpenAI turn fan identity into an image prompt.
items.tickerTitle: Messi and OpenAI turn fan identity into an image prompt
items.summary: A watch note on the reported Messi x OpenAI campaign and why sports identity is an obvious stage for generative images.
items.xPost: If Messi x OpenAI is the first global football season of the AI era, the interesting part is not the haircut. It is fan identity becoming editable media: colors, loyalty, image prompts, and culture as a shared generative surface.

items.title: Apple Intelligence still has a naming problem.
items.tickerTitle: Apple's AI problem is naming before delivery
items.summary: Apple's AI brand is elegant, but the delivery gap makes every Siri miss feel bigger than a normal product delay.
items.xPost: Apple may have named the thing too loudly. Apple Intelligence sounds inevitable. But if Siri still feels ordinary, every miss becomes a brand problem, not just a feature gap.

items.title: AI data centers in orbit sound absurd until the compute hunger looks ordinary.
items.tickerTitle: SpaceX wants AI data centers in orbit
items.summary: Orbital compute is easy to mock and hard to dismiss, because the AI infrastructure appetite keeps getting less earthly.
items.xPost: AI data centers in space sound ridiculous in the exact way infrastructure shifts often sound ridiculous before they become spreadsheets. The appeal is not the spectacle. It is the visible hunger for compute.

items.title: The AI bubble question is really an infrastructure question.
items.tickerTitle: AI data centers turn the bubble debate into an energy debate
items.summary: The simple bubble take misses the physical story: power, cooling, chips, grids, data centers and useful intelligence per watt.
items.xPost: I do not buy the simple AI bubble take. Some valuations can be overheated. Some demos can be theater. But the deeper story is physical: power, cooling, chips, land, grids, data centers. The real question is useful intelligence per watt.

items.title: Vibe coding is leverage, not a replacement for judgment.
items.tickerTitle: Vibe coding works best when the human still knows the craft
items.summary: AI-assisted programming is strongest in expert hands: faster exploration, faster drafts, but still dependent on taste, structure and review.
items.xPost: Vibe coding without engineering judgment is mostly confidence cosplay. Vibe coding with expertise is leverage: faster drafts, faster exploration, and still enough taste to catch the structural mistakes.
